LAW AND MOTION 301, JUDGE BUSCH MADE A DISCLOSURE ABOUT 11 YEARS AGO HE REPRESENTED THE INSURANCE COMMISSIONER IN THE EXECUTIVE LIFE CASE AND HAD WORKED WITH MR. WELSH AND MISS LARSEN AND DOES NOT VIEW THIS AS A BASIS FOR RECUSAL. NO OBJECTIONS WERE MADE. APPLICANT INSURANCE COMMISSIONER DAVE JONES' MOTION FOR ORDER APPROVING REHABILITATION PLAN FOR MAJESTIC INSURANCE COMPANY IS GRANTED. THE COMMISSIONER MADE AN ADEQUATE SHOWING OF THE RESULT OF THE PLAN AND THE REHABILITATION PLAN IS LAWFUL AND FAVORABLE TO PROTECT THE INTERESTS OF MAJESTIC POLICYHOLDERS, CREDITORS OR SHAREHOLDERS. PER DIEM FEES ASSESSED BY THE COURT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER, CAROL KAREN CSR # 8189

MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. THOMAS WELSH & ATTY. CYNTHIA J. LARSEN OF THE ORRICK LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (916) 329-7970 APPEARING FOR THE DEPARTMENT OF INSURANCE;
ATTY. KRISTIAN D. WHITTEN, DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL, TEL. NO. 703-5589 APPEARING FOR THE DEPARTMENT OF INSURANCE;
ATTY. ROBERT N. BERG OF THE MICHELMAN & ROBINSON LLP, TEL. NO. 882-7770 APPEARING FOR THE MAJESTIC INSURANCE COMPANY;
ATTY. CRAIG S. BLOOMGARTEN OF MANNATT, PHELPS & PHILLIPS LLP, TEL. NO. (310) 312-4240 APPEARING FOR THE NY WORKERS COMPENSATION BOARD (OBJECTOR);
ATTY. GLEN VANDYKE OF THE VAN DYKE LAW GROUP, TEL. NO. (530) 587-2130 APPEARING FOR THE OBJECTOR, MARK TANNER CONSTRUCTION, INCORPORATED BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE

RULING - JUDGE BUSCH MADE A DISCLOSURE ABOUT 11 YEARS AGO HE REPRESENTED THE INSURANCE COMMISSIONER IN THE EXECUTIVE LIFE CASE AND HAD WORKED WITH MR. WELSH AND MISS LARSEN AND DOES NOT VIEW THIS AS A BASIS FOR RECUSAL. NO OBJECTIONS WERE MADE. APPLICANT INSURANCE COMMISSIONER DAVE JONES' MOTION FOR ORDER APPROVING REHABILITATION PLAN FOR MAJESTIC INSURANCE COMPANY IS GRANTED. THE COMMISSIONER MADE AN ADEQUATE SHOWING OF THE RESULT OF THE PLAN AND THE REHABILITATION PLAN IS LAWFUL AND FAVORABLE TO PROTECT THE INTERESTS OF MAJESTIC POLICYHOLDERS, CREDITORS OR SHAREHOLDERS. PER DIEM FEES ASSESSED BY THE COURT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter, Carol Karen CSR # 8189 =301/PJB
